title: Fetch-to-Commit Ingestion Unit, Checkpoints, and Partial Failure · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A nonprofit investigative newsroom and participating publishers. They need conditional fetching, strict byte and parser budgets, constrained PostgreSQL records, reversible migrations, idempotent versioned entries, and exact fetch-to-commit reconciliation. Planned artifact: Deliver Publisher-Friendly Transactional Ingestion Service with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Store and send ETag and Last-Modified validators correctly, interpret 304 without parsing a body, and update validators only from admitted responses.
Encode nullability, checks, unique scopes, foreign keys, cascades, timestamps, states, and length budgets in PostgreSQL.
Stage additive schema, backfill, dual-read or dual-write, validation, cutover, cleanup, and old-version compatibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Feed Go Validator Precedence
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Go team handles case 9292 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile fetch, parse, normalize, atomic commit, validators, checkpoints, and post-commit effects; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Store and send ETag and Last-Modified validators correctly, interpret 304 without parsing a body, and update validators only from admitted responses.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Feed Go Validator Precedence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for store and send etag and last-modified validators correctly, interpret 304 without parsing a body, and update validators only from admitted responses. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Feed Go Validator Precedence, keep Feed Go Validator Precedence connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
